active_denominator: BEP-Deep: 972 fixtures, 418 positives, 554 negative controls (including 418 paired repair controls)
bep_max_validation: A010 BEP-Max adversarial validation keeps the locked BEP-Deep denominator unchanged and checks 4306 generated cases, 418 contrastive repair pairs, 418 local repair frontiers, certificate/repair edges in the exposure graph, and complete validation ladders for all 25 issue classes; it is a validation burden, not a relabeling or prevalence expansion.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim live-web prevalence or deployed-site vulnerability rates.
Do not claim complete browser conformance or full browser-security soundness.
Do not claim human developer intent beyond explicit source text.
Do not claim external baselines ran when wrappers record unavailable or unsupported status.
Do not rank scanners as universally better or worse outside the declared semantic task.
latex_source_integrity: A012 adds a source-level LaTeX integrity check: exactly one approved IEEEtran documentclass, exactly one end-of-document marker, no active prose after that marker, and no paper-source traces of internal process artifacts. It changes only source hygiene and paper presentation, not labels or results.
paper_thesis: Browser-enforced security policies can drift from explicit developer-facing intent because delivery disposition, fallback/default behavior, request/cache/rendering context, browser state, ordered policy layers, and cross-policy composition determine the browser-effective judgement. BEP-Deep evaluates that drift with source-grounded fixtures, repair-paired negative controls, an independent decision-table oracle, semantic mutation adequacy, exact witness minimality, and a typed effective-exposure graph.

positive_claims.claim: Policy intent drift is a cross-layer semantic relation among explicit public intent, generated policy surface, and browser-effective judgement.
positive_claims.evidence: formal core + 45 source-grounded claims + 45 source-span closure rows + 35 rules + 116 base fixtures + 972 BEP-Deep fixtures

positive_claims.claim: Minimal semantic conflict witnesses are a reusable debugging and evaluation object for browser-policy engineering.
positive_claims.evidence: 418 positive witnesses; 418 exact header-subset minimality certificates; 418 validated counterfactual repairs; 418 paired repair negative controls; independent decision-table agreement over 972 fixtures

positive_claims.claim: Checklist/header-presence and single-policy baselines miss classes that depend on delivery disposition, fallback/default behavior, request/rendering context, browser state, and cross-policy composition.
positive_claims.evidence: 322 semantic positives missed by the header-presence control; 28/28 semantic mutants killed; ablation losses over 13 semantic components; effective-exposure graph paths

positive_claims.claim: The approach is CPU-native and reproducible with public specifications, documentation, and deterministic fixtures.
positive_claims.evidence: BEP-Deep protocol amendments A006-A011, source snapshots, checksums, deterministic scripts, source-span closure, clean package

positive_claims.claim: BEP-Max strengthens oracle/workload independence by surrounding BEP-Deep with adversarial validation cases, contrastive repair pairs, local repair-frontier checks, and issue-class validation ladders.
positive_claims.evidence: 4306/4306 adversarial validation cases pass; 418/418 contrastive repair pairs pass; 418/418 local repair frontiers certified; 25/25 issue classes have complete validation ladders.

post_lock_amendment: Any denominator, metric, label, baseline, ablation, RQ, oracle-validation, or mutation-adequacy change requires a protocol_amendments.csv entry and cannot be silently folded into locked results.
post_lock_change_policy: Any denominator, metric, label, baseline, ablation, RQ, oracle-validation, or mutation-adequacy change requires a protocol_amendments.csv entry and cannot be silently folded into locked results.
validation_report_closure: Release-level validation reports and auxiliary source ledgers must resolve to the same 45 admitted claims and 15 admitted sources as the source-span and fixture validation gates.
